site.name: Dr. Şadi İdem
site.title: Dr. Şadi İdem — Pediatrician
site.description: Functional pediatrics in Eskişehir: newborn care, development, allergy and asthma follow-up with a whole-child, family-centered approach.

floating.whatsAppPrefill: Hello, I'd like information about an appointment.

megaMenu.introBody: Pediatric care and child health follow-up in Eskişehir, with a preventive approach and clear communication with families.

hero.title: By your child's side, <accent>through every milestone.</accent>
hero.description: From newborn to adolescence, with a warm and child-centered approach. Vaccination, development assessment, allergy and asthma management.

journeyTimeline.title: Our clinical journey
journeyTimeline.subtitle: From training to today’s approach — scroll and watch the accent move along the line.

entries.title: 1996 — Beginning medical school
entries.body: Foundational years linking basic science with clinical observation. Interest in child health grew steadily during this period.

entries.title: 2002 — Pediatrics specialty training
entries.body: Specialty training in child health and diseases; depth in newborn care, developmental assessment, and common pediatric conditions.

entries.title: 2005 — Clinical work in Eskişehir
entries.body: Ongoing practice in the region; long-term relationships with families, immunization programs, and focused allergy–asthma follow-up.

entries.title: Today — Whole-child, family-centered care
entries.body: With more than twenty-nine years of experience, we support children’s health through education, parent workshops, and an integrative mindset.

about.title: 29 years with you
about.biography: My interests include newborn care, child and adolescent development and psychology, parent-child relationships, allergic diseases, asthma, allergic rhinitis, ecology and child development. I am married and a father of two. I give seminars titled “Liberating Child-Centered Parenting.”

topics.title: Baby & Child
topics.subtitle: This section is for education only; it is not a diagnosis, treatment plan, or prescription. For definitive care, consult your child’s regular clinician.

articleTitles.sik-hastalanan-cocuk: The child who gets sick often
articleTitles.cocugumuz-ne-kadar-su-tuketmeli: How much water should my child drink?

articleExcerpts.aile-ici-iliskiler-ve-cocuk-sagligi: How relationships at home affect a child's physical and emotional wellbeing.
articleExcerpts.atesli-cocuga-yaklasim: What to watch for when your child has a fever and when to seek care.
articleExcerpts.atesli-havale: What febrile seizures are, why they happen, and how families should respond.
articleExcerpts.sik-hastalanan-cocuk: Immune development, daycare impact, and when frequent illnesses are normal.
articleExcerpts.tamamlayici-beslenmenin-ilkeleri: Starting solids after 6 months — timing, foods, and how to begin safely.
articleExcerpts.yurutec-faydali-mi-zararli-mi: Motor development considerations and safety when using walkers.
articleExcerpts.anne-sutu-ve-emzirme: Why breastfeeding matters, latch basics, and common questions answered.
articleExcerpts.istahsiz-cocuk-ve-yemek-secme: Why kids refuse foods and how to build peaceful meal habits at home.
articleExcerpts.cocugumuz-ne-kadar-su-tuketmeli: Daily fluid needs by age and signs your child may need more fluids.
articleExcerpts.bebeklerde-uyku-sorunu: How sleep changes by age and frequent causes of sleep struggles.
articleExcerpts.uyku-egitimi-nasil-verebilirim: Gentle routines that support better sleep without harsh methods.
articleExcerpts.cocuga-asi-yaptirmali-miyim: Evidence-based perspective on vaccine safety and benefits.

approach.title: Liberating, child-centered pediatrics
approach.body: Every child is unique. I believe in listening before treating, understanding the family's lifestyle, and deciding together. I share this philosophy through my “Liberating Child-Centered Parenting” seminars.

testimonials.title: Your voices matter
testimonials.subtitle: Words from families who walked this journey with us.

functionalMedicinePage.metaDescription: Seven pillars of functional medicine approach to pediatric care — nutrition, gut health, lifestyle and root-cause thinking.
functionalMedicinePage.title: Functional medicine in pediatrics
functionalMedicinePage.lead: Health is rebuilt beyond symptoms — at the cellular and lifestyle level. Below are the seven pillars we use to guide individualized care.

visual.ctaTitle: Ready to plan an individualized conversation for your child? We’re here.

parallax.title: Dr. Şadi İdem — Pediatric specialist
parallax.subtitle: In Eskişehir — well-child visits, immunizations, and allergy–asthma care with holistic, child-centered pediatrics.

intro.title: Pediatric specialist Dr. Şadi İdem in Eskişehir
intro.description: I focus on monitoring, assessment and clear communication with families. From the newborn period through childhood, I offer follow-up and counselling tailored to each stage of life.

services.title: Our services
services.subtitle: Our services focus on monitoring, assessment and education in child health. Each child’s care is planned around individual needs.
services.disclaimer: Our service areas are not promotional claims for diagnosis or treatment. All assessments take place within physician examination and clinical evaluation.
services.hubBody: With over 18 years of experience I support your child’s healthy growth, combining modern pediatrics with a functional medicine perspective for each child.

doctorNote.body: In more than 25 years of practice I've seen that a healthy child grows not only through medicine, but through proper nutrition, a healthy environment and strong parent-child bonds. In my clinic I assess your child as a whole, identifying individualized root causes to aim for lasting improvement.

approach.label: Approach
approach.title: Clinical approach
approach.description: We emphasize regular monitoring when children are healthy, not only during illness, with family education at the center.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Common questions about visits and follow-up.
faq.disclaimer: General information only — not a substitute for an in-person medical visit.

items.q: How often should healthy children be seen?
items.a: Intervals depend on age and development; growth, nutrition and general health are reviewed on a schedule.

items.q: What is assessed in newborn follow-up?
items.a: Feeding, weight gain, jaundice monitoring, sleep and overall clinical status.
Intervals are set according to corrected age, nutrition, growth and clinical needs.

items.q: At what ages is sleep counseling useful?
items.a: From infancy onward, sleep routines can be reviewed in an age-appropriate way.
Position, latch, weight gain and how the mother is adapting to the process.

items.q: What should I bring to a visit?
items.a: Previous reports, medication lists and the vaccination card are helpful.
Only when clinically appropriate, based on the physician's assessment.

items.q: How do I book an appointment?
items.a: Use the contact form, phone or WhatsApp on our contact page.

carousel.title: Featured service areas
carousel.subtitle: Newborn care, well-child visits, clinical assessment and allergy management.

healthCorner.title: Health corner
healthCorner.subtitle: Articles on child health, nutrition, vaccines and pediatrics.

childMonitoring.title: Well-child care and preventive pediatrics
childMonitoring.lead: Age-appropriate growth, development, nutrition and vaccination planning with transparent family communication.
childMonitoring.ctaBody: Let's plan the right follow-up for your child together.

items.q: How do I book an appointment?
items.a: Use the appointment form on our site or call 0 (555) 151 1330. Outside emergencies, office hours are preferred.

items.q: How often should my child be seen?
items.a: Newborns need frequent visits early on; later we follow growth and vaccines on a schedule. Healthy children still benefit from periodic developmental checks.

items.q: What should I do if my child has a fever?
items.a: Fever is part of the body's defense. Guidance on fluids, antipyretics and red flags is summarized in our article “Approach to the Febrile Child.”
Vaccines are among the most studied and effective preventive tools. Ask us in clinic for personalized counseling.
Yes — submit the online form and we will call you to confirm a convenient time.
